primary.evidence: Live probe of the deployed worker for TWO on-curve controls (foundation wallet J8ez8GMs… and System Program 1111…1111) both returned balance_read_failed with diagnostic '403 Forbidden: {"error":{"code":403,"message":"Your IP or provider is blocked from this endpoint"}}', rpcSource=secret_binding.
primary.meaning: The SOLANA_RPC_URL secret IS bound, but the endpoint it points to rejects the Cloudflare Worker's egress IP/provider (403). Public api.mainnet-beta.solana.com and some providers block cloud/Worker IPs. ALL reads fail at the rpc_query stage until the secret points at a provider that permits Cloudflare Worker egress (e.g. Helius/Triton/QuickNode with Worker egress allowed).
primary.fixOwner: operator (infra) — re-set SOLANA_RPC_URL to a Worker-permitting endpoint; no code change can bypass a provider IP block.

secondary.evidence: The operator's treasury query (5Cohfz… , a Squads multisig PDA = off-curve) returned diagnostic:"" (empty). getAssociatedTokenAddress used allowOwnerOffCurve=false, so splGetAssociatedTokenAddressSync threw TokenOwnerOffCurveError (empty .message) DURING ATA derivation, before any RPC call — hence empty diagnostic. This masked the underlying RPC 403.
secondary.fixOwner: code (this report) — read path now derives the ATA with allowOwnerOffCurve=true so PDA owners are queryable, and the route never returns an empty diagnostic.

codeChanges: src/lib/lifePlusSolana.ts: getAssociatedTokenAddress gains allowOwnerOffCurve param (default false); readLifePlusBalanceForOwner derives the ATA with allowOwnerOffCurve=true so off-curve PDA owners (treasury) are queryable. On-curve wallets unaffected.

app/api/solana/lifepp-balance/route.ts: classifyBalanceReadError maps failures to safe codes (rpc_403_forbidden | rpc_429_rate_limited | rpc_timeout | rpc_request_failed | rpc_invalid_response | owner_has_no_token_account | invalid_owner_pubkey | token_account_query_failed); never returns an empty diagnostic; returns curated secret-free message + errorClass (class name only); raw provider body is NOT echoed; URL-shaped text is redacted; full detail logged server-side only (wrangler tail).
Added P3A debug-safe response fields: rpcSource, cluster, lifePlusMint, ownerAddressShort, balanceReadStage, diagnosticCode.

expectedPostDeployBehavior.normalOnCurveWalletNoLife: ok=true, rawBalance="0" (ATA not found -> 0n) — ONCE the RPC 403 is resolved.
expectedPostDeployBehavior.treasuryOffCurvePda: now queryable (allowOwnerOffCurve=true): ok=true rawBalance=<n> if it holds LIFE++, else "0" — ONCE the RPC 403 is resolved. Until then both return diagnosticCode=rpc_403_forbidden (clear, non-empty).
expectedPostDeployBehavior.note: These code fixes are NOT deployed (no deploy in this phase). The deployed worker (version 8cde4a3c) still returns the old shape until the operator redeploys.

nextSmokeCommands: # After operator re-sets SOLANA_RPC_URL to a Worker-permitting provider AND redeploys ahin-io-p3a:
# If still failing, the JSON now carries diagnosticCode (e.g. rpc_403_forbidden) — never empty; check `wrangler tail` for URL-redacted server detail.
operatorNextAction: Re-set the RPC secret to an endpoint that permits Cloudflare Worker egress: `npx wrangler secret put SOLANA_RPC_URL --config wrangler.workers.p3a.jsonc` (a paid Helius/Triton/QuickNode endpoint, NOT api.mainnet-beta.solana.com). Then rebuild+redeploy ahin-io-p3a so these diagnostic improvements ship. No code change can bypass a provider-side IP block.
boundaries: Diagnostics + readonly-path only. No transfer/burn/signing/canary/public-payment enabled; mint + treasury constants unchanged and immutable; no deploy/Wrangler/workflow/secret-set/flag-flip; root ahin.io untouched; P3B blocked.
nextPhase: Operator resolves the RPC 403 (Worker-permitting endpoint) + redeploys; re-run smoke. P3B remains BLOCKED.
